description_long: This object is a freestanding basketball hoop structure, typically found in urban parks or recreational areas. It features a rectangular, weathered backboard with a visible square target area, mounted on a sturdy, dark-colored metal pole. The hoop is made of metal and is attached to the backboard, with a white net hanging from it. The base of the pole is broad and reinforced, suggesting stability and resistance to tipping. The materials and finish indicate it is designed for outdoor use, with surfaces that can withstand exposure to the elements. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and function over decorative elements.
description: A robust outdoor basketball hoop with a rectangular backboard, metal pole, and netted rim, designed for urban recreational use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular backboard, hoop, and net, with the supporting pole centered beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the backboard, the protruding hoop, and the angled support arm connecting to the pole.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the backboard, the supporting pole, and the base, with no hoop visible.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing the hoop, net, and the structural connection between the backboard and the pole.
